events.citation: monument rear verbatim: '…assisted in repulsing Pickett's Charge.' (Stone Sentinels 1st New York Battery K page, fetched 2026-07-08); page narrative: ordered into line at the gallop at the height of the charge, firing 89 rounds (57 percussion shell, 15 shrapnel, 17 time shell)
events.note: opens on the inferred gallop-in arrival ~15:18

events.citation: Fry, 'Pettigrew's Charge at Gettysburg', SHSP v7 pp.91-93: 'All of the five regimental colors of my command reached the line of the enemy's works' - the brigade fought to the wall (track kf t=8580 documented)
events.note: Attached at BRIGADE grain: Fry attests 'my command' as a whole; no per-regiment fire attestation exists, so his five children carry no musketry events (attach-level discipline - never the same fire at two family levels). Window: from the Emmitsburg Road crossing on the track spine (t=8340) to the brigade wrecked at the wall (t=8700). Library caveats: Fry was down wounded before the end; his account contests Virginia-centric readings - kept, not reconciled.

events.citation: Hall's OR report (OR 27/1): the wall line engages the approach, regiments then crowd right toward the Copse (track kfs t=8700/9000, inferred)
events.note: Regiment-grain window inferred on the brigade's attested spine; parent us-hall carries no event (attach-level discipline). Opens as the column crosses the Emmitsburg Road on the track spine (t=8340); ends with the penetration collapsed (t=9000).

events.citation: Hall's OR report (OR 27/1): at the wall immediately south of the Copse as Kemper's and Armistead's fronts close; the bolt documented - the battalion 'inexplicably bolted for the rear' at the climax, uncovering Cowan's guns (track kf t=8700, documented)
events.note: Window ENDS at the documented bolt (t=8700) - no fire authored past it; the rally (t=9000) returns the unit to the line after the crisis.

events.citation: Hall's OR report (OR 27/1): the wall line engages, then Hall shifts his regiments right/north toward the Copse at the break (track kfs t=8700/9000, inferred); Haskell 1908
events.note: Regiment-grain window inferred on the brigade's attested spine; parent us-hall carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: massed rifle fire from Hays's line repulses Pettigrew's front (ABT maps; track kfs inferred); Col. Sherrill mortally wounded during the repulse (Stone Sentinels, track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: Attached at BRIGADE grain - Sherrill's brigade is undecomposed (display-LOD roster); the record gives no finer attestation. Fire window itself is a modern reconstruction (ABT maps), hence inferred despite the documented casualty inside it.

events.citation: massed rifle fire repulses Marshall's and Lane's front at the wall (ABT maps; track kf t=8700 inferred); Marshall's road crossing 'under canister and rifle fire from Hays's line' (ABT 3:30-3:45 PM map, csa-marshall kf t=8340)
events.note: Attached at BRIGADE grain - undecomposed (display-LOD roster). The regiment-track survey notes 12th NJ / 1st DE fence volleys as a future decomposition target; until those regiments exist as units there is nothing finer to attach to.

events.citation: Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305: Stannard's order 'Change front, forward on first company' - each company 'saw in their immediate front not sixty yards away General Pickett's command... and at once opened a deadly fire on their flank'; 13th VT monument: 'changed front forward on first company, advanced 200 yards, attacking the Confederate right flank' (track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: REGIMENT grain (Sturtevant narrates the 13th); parent us-stannard carries no event (attach-level discipline). Window rides the wheel keyframes (t=8400 wheel under way -> t=8700 documented firing position), ends with the repulse sealed (t=9000). Clock time ~15:20+ is inferred - the pass-1 library flags Stannard's maneuver as not clock-attested by any witness.

events.citation: Sturtevant 1910: the 14th essentially static through the crisis, firing into Kemper's front as the assault obliques past (track kf t=8700, inferred)
events.note: Regiment-grain frontal fire inferred on the brigade's attested engagement; parent us-stannard carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: Sturtevant 1910 pp.304-305 (the brigade's flank fire, 'a deadly fire on their flank'); Veazey's OR report: on the 13th's left firing into the right flank of Pickett's column (track kf t=8700)
events.note: REGIMENT grain; parent us-stannard carries no event (attach-level discipline). Wheels out with the 13th (t=8400), forming on its left; window ends with the repulse sealed (t=9000). Clock time inferred (pass-1 library: Stannard's maneuver not clock-attested).

events.citation: canister into Fry's and Marshall's front at the wall - modern reconstruction (ABT maps; track kf t=8700 inferred)
events.note: Climax window ~15:20-15:30 on the assault's track spine; no witness times Arnold's canister fire - inferred throughout.

events.citation: Stone Sentinels 1st NY Independent Battery page / Andrew Cowan: the 'double canister at ten yards' repulse in front of the Copse (track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: The ten-yards blast sits at kf t=8700 (~15:25); window brackets it across the climax ~15:20-15:30, edges inferred. The 59th NY's documented bolt (t=8700) uncovered these guns at exactly the blast's moment.

events.citation: Fuger, 'Cushing's Battery at Gettysburg', JMSI v41 (1907) p.408, verbatim via NPS Hartwig 2012: 'We continued to fire double and treble charges of our canister'; Haskell 1908: 'Cushing, firing almost his last canister, had dropped dead among his guns'
events.note: Window ends at Cushing's death and the battery's effective destruction (kf t=8700, documented, ~15:20-15:25; remaining guns run to the wall at kf t=8580). Library flag carried: Fuger is a contested, self-interested retelling - the canister passage is its most solidly verified element (matches the 1907 JMSI wording via NPS's direct quote); Haskell's 'shot through the head' vs Fuger's 'shot in the mouth' stays unreconciled.

events.citation: overrun documented: Capt. Rorty killed when Kemper's Virginians briefly overran the battery (Stone Sentinels 1st NY Battery B page; track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: Canister into the final approach is inferred - the overrun attests close action, not a fire window; window ends at the overrun (t=8700), guns recovered by t=9000 on the track.

events.citation: tablet verbatim: '…opened with canister at short range on the advancing Confederates.' (Stone Sentinels 5th US Artillery Battery C page, fetched 2026-07-08)
events.note: canister window aligned with the climax phase (8400–9000), matching the modeled II Corps batteries

events.citation: fire into Davis's and Pettigrew's front from Ziegler's Grove reconstructed (ABT maps); Woodruff mortally wounded during the repulse - Hazard's report via Iron Brigader (track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: Window inferred around the documented casualty; Davis's advance breaks short of the wall under this sector's fire on the track spine (csa-davis kf t=8580).

events.citation: McDermott 1889: the regiment never leaves the wall; Cos. I and A change front against the flankers as Armistead crosses (track kf t=8700 documented); Haskell 1908 (the Angle crisis)
events.note: The anchor of the Angle musketry - static at the wall between the Angle and the Copse; t0=8580 as Pickett's front closes on the track spine; siblings ride the same spine at inferred confidence. Parent us-webb carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: Smith's OR report: at the outer-angle wall as the column closes, ~50 men helping serve Cushing's guns; the two-step fallback at the breakthrough (track kf t=8700 documented)
events.note: Fire window inferred on the 69th's spine; the regiment keeps firing from its stepped-back position through the sealing of the breakthrough (t=9000). Parent us-webb carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: Rawley Martin, SHSP v32 pp.183-195: Armistead 'scaled the wall' crying 'Boys, give them the cold steel!' - the 53rd, the brigade's center regiment, crossed with him (track kf t=8700 documented); Fuger (JMSI v41 via NPS Hartwig): Armistead's men 'landing right in the middle of our Battery', hand-to-hand
events.note: REGIMENT grain: the 53rd VA is the attested crossing regiment; parent csa-armistead carries no event and no other Armistead child has fire attestation (attach-level discipline). Window: closing on the Angle (t=8640) through the sealed breakthrough (t=9000, unit routed on the track). Library flag: Martin is a 34-year recollection.

events.citation: Waitt 1906 (19th MA history): Devereux asks Hancock's leave and throws the 19th MA and 42nd NY into the gap at the Copse; hand-to-hand at the clump until the penetration collapses
events.note: REGIMENT grain (Waitt narrates the 19th); parent us-hall carries no event (attach-level discipline). t0=8700 is the plunge on the track spine - the position keyframe is inferred, the episode documented in Waitt.

events.citation: the plunge beside the 19th MA on Devereux's initiative with Hancock's verbal leave (Waitt 1906; Hall's OR report; track kfs inferred)
events.note: The 42nd's own fire is not separately attested - inferred from the documented paired plunge; parent us-hall carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: Haskell 1908 - CONTESTED, tracked reading: brought up to the crest and fires from it, not advancing when Webb ordered (track kf t=8700 documented); the final advance to the wall ~15:35 (track kf t=9300)
events.note: The 72nd's conduct is the Haskell-vs-veterans dispute (the regiment's veterans disputed his Angle characterization - library flag); the tracked reading has it firing from the crest, then advancing as the penetration collapses; window ends at the advance (t=9300). Parent us-webb carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: regiments crowd north toward the Copse to meet the breakthrough; close-range fight at the Copse (Haskell 1908; ABT 3:45-4:00 PM map; track kfs inferred)
events.note: Attached at BRIGADE grain - Harrow's brigade is undecomposed (display-LOD roster); the record gives no finer attestation.

events.citation: Sturtevant 1910: front refused south from its position, joining the 16th's fire against Wilcox and Lang's advance (track kf t=10200, inferred)
events.note: Regiment-grain window inferred alongside the 16th's documented reversal; parent us-stannard carries no event (attach-level discipline).

events.citation: Veazey's OR report: the 16th's reversal - strikes the left flank of Wilcox and Lang's supporting advance (track kf t=10380 documented)
events.note: Window brackets the documented strike (kf t=10380) between the re-form facing south (track kfs t=9900-10200) and Wilcox's/Lang's withdrawal ~15:55 (their tracks, t=10500); window edges inferred around the documented event.

events.citation: the battery's two-attack record (reconstruction/dossiers/us-btty-fitzhugh.md EC5): the first attack 'collapsed in 30-45 min of his fire'; the second (Wilcox/Lang) 'broken by enfilade' — window = the Wilcox/Lang follow-up spine (10200-10500), matching us-btty-phillips-florida-repulse and us-btty-hart-second-line on the same ground
events.note: ROUNDS ITEMIZED across both windows: 57 percussion shell + 15 shrapnel + 17 time shell = 89 (or-27-1-fitzhugh via dossier) [B: ~8-10 min of full-battery fire total] — the physical tiebreaker that ruled his move-clock conflict toward ~15:00 (13:00-vs-15:00 carried on the unit)

events.citation: marker verbatim: 'A second line advancing was met with double canister which dispersed it.' (Stone Sentinels 15th New York Independent Battery page, Hancock Avenue marker, fetched 2026-07-08)
events.note: the marker does not name the formation — window aligned with the Wilcox/Lang follow-up spine, matching Phillips's Florida-brigade attestation on the same ground

events.citation: tablet verbatim: 'A charge was made within the range of the battery immediately afterwards by the Florida brigade and at about the same time a Confederate battery opened on the left front which at once received the concentrated fire of the batteries of the brigade driving the cannoneers from their guns which they abandoned.' (Stone Sentinels 5th Massachusetts Battery page, Hancock Avenue tablet, fetched 2026-07-08)
events.note: window = the Wilcox/Lang follow-up spine (us-14vt-wilcox-front / us-16vt-wilcox-flank, 10200–10500)

events.citation: Wheeler's OR report verbatim: 'This gave me a fine opportunity to enfilade their column with canister, which threw them into great disorder, and brought them to a halt three times.' (transcribed at 13nybattery.com/battles/wheeler_gettys.htm, VERIFIED survey §2.2)
events.note: his ~4 p.m. clock sits at the window's very end — encoded tight against it per the plan (t1 = endTime); the fight's post-16:00 remainder is out of slice; DAY-EXPANSION SLICE 1: the post-16:00 remainder now modeled — t1 10800 -> 11400 (inferred ~10 min: his stated 'about 4 p.m.' arrival is profiled -35 [-55,0] and his three-halt enfilade reads mid-fight; the remainder is bounded short because the assault had collapsed — the alternative clock-error reading (arrival mid-repulse) stays open for a re-timing pass, us-btty-wheeler.md Conflicts §1)

events.citation: 'firing only four rounds of long range solid shot' after taking position (or-27-1-mccartney No. 238, verbatim, PRIMARY own-report; reconstruction/dossiers/us-btty-mccartney.md EC5.1)
events.note: the fire is report-attested; the ~5-minute window ~16:05-16:10 is inferred (four deliberate long-range rounds shortly after the ~16:00 arrival) — minutes-grade, LOW intensity

environment.note: unattested; light SW breeze on a hot July afternoon - smoke drifts toward the NE (windTowardDeg is the drift-TOWARD bearing, battle-format.md 'Environment'). The research corpus carries no July 3 wind claim; likeliest attestation path is Michael Jacobs's meteorological register - he is already a verified, habitually precise source in the library (Notes on the Rebel Invasion, 1864) and kept regular thermometer/wind readings; a targeted research fetch would upgrade this to documented. No citation field on purpose: nothing attests these values yet.
